q: How many people depend on marine and coastal biodiversity for their livelihoods?
The loss of biodiversity affects the lives of more than 1 billion people living in which environment?
Which of the following is NOT one of the five major threats to biodiversity mentioned?
What is defined as the situation where habitats become divided into small areas?
Which type of species is more sensitive to reduction in habitat area?
Which activity is listed as a major reason for habitat destruction?
Shifting cultivation in upland areas of Myanmar is an example of ___.
What is the most significant cause of biodiversity loss globally?
Which of the following threatens the agrobiodiversity of Myanmar?
a: Replacement of local landraces with modern varieties

q: What causes pollution in soil, water, or air according to the text?
What does IAS stand for?
How can IAS be introduced unintentionally?
Which of the following is an invasive alien plant species in Myanmar?
___ indicates the loss of identity of wild plant species as a result of transfer of genes from crop plants.
Gene flow is expected to have its greatest effect on which species?
a: Weed species closely related to crop plants

q: Why is the introduction of genetically engineered (GE) organisms considered a dangerous global experiment?
a: Because it is virtually impossible to recall them back once released
Because they only live for one day

q: What term is also used for overexploitation?
Overfishing is the removal of fish at a rate greater than the species can ___.
Overhunting threatens what fraction of endangered vertebrates?
Which of the following is NOT listed as a zoonotic virus linked to bushmeat or wildlife trade?
Which factor is described as a major factor in the distribution of species across the globe?
What does human overpopulation lead to?
a: Increased demand for water and housing

q: The trade of live or dead individuals, tissues, skins, bones, and feathers is called ___.
Which animal is the most trafficked in the world?
What are 'wildlife trade hotspots'?
a: Places where wildlife trade is banned
Places where targeted action for protection is particularly worthwhile

q: What is CITES?
a: The Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Flora and Fauna
